# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD to Production

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: production-build
          path: build/ # Adjust this path to your actual build output directory

  deploy-to-production:
    needs: build-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Download build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: production-build
          path: ./build/ # Adjust this path to where you want the artifact downloaded

      - name: Deploy to production (example: rsync over SSH)
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_USERNAME }}
          key: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY }}
          script: |
            # Example deployment steps:
            # 1. Stop service
            # 2. Backup current production files (optional but recommended)
            # 3. Remove old files
            # 4. Copy new build files
            # 5. Install dependencies if needed (e.g., node_modules)
            # 6. Run database migrations (if applicable)
            # 7. Start service

            # Create a temporary directory for deployment
            mkdir -p /tmp/deploy_app
            # Copy downloaded artifact to server
            rsync -avz --delete ./build/ ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_APP_PATH }}/
            # Example: Restart a PM2 service
            # cd ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_APP_PATH }}
            # npm install --production # if you didn't include node_modules in build
            # pm2 reload your_app_name || pm2 start dist/server.js --name your_app_name # Adjust based on your app
            echo "Deployment to production successful!"

      # - name: Deploy to production (example: Docker Compose)
      #   uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3
      #   with:
      #     host: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_HOST }}
      #     username: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_USERNAME }}
      #     key: ${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY }}
      #     script: |
      #       cd /path/to/your/docker-compose-files
      #       docker-compose pull
      #       docker-compose up -d --remove-orphans

      # - name: Deploy to production (example: cloud provider CLI)
      #   run: |
      #     # e.g., for AWS S3/CloudFront
      #     aws s3 sync ./build/ s3://${{ secrets.AWS_S3_BUCKET }} --delete
      #     aws cloudfront create-invalidation --distribution-id ${{ secrets.AWS_CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID }} --paths "/*"
      #   env:
      #     AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
      #     AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
      #     AWS_REGION: us-east-1 # Or your specific region
